summary: This candidate focuses on checking the Method setting and relating it to the documented threshold basis options of count, cube, or a mixture of both.
rough_steps: Open or view the Tote Exchange Settings interface. [Image support needed: screenshot of the Tote Exchange Settings page with the Method field visible.]
Locate the Method field on the screen.
Read the displayed method option and compare it to the documented choices: Count, Cube, or Cube and Count.
Use the surrounding threshold fields to confirm that the screen includes count-based and cube-based threshold entries referenced by the source.
Record which threshold basis is configured without inferring any undocumented software behavior beyond the listed options.
expected_result: The user can identify whether tote exchange is configured to use count, cube, or a combined cube and count basis according to the documented Method field.
failure_or_escalation_notes: Escalate if the Method field is missing or shows a value not documented in the source.
Do not infer exact calculation logic for the combined method because the source only states that it allows a mixture of both.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Tote Exchange Settings interface
Aveva API page view

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The software has the ability to do it based off counts. And so the method is either counts cube or ... a mixture of both.
OCR lists Method with options Count, Cube, Cube and Count.

extraction_notes: This is kept separate from the broader screen-interpretation candidate because the source explicitly calls out method selection as a distinct concept.
No setting-change steps are included because the source does not provide edit or save actions.

title: Identify Who Can Update Tote Exchange Settings And Note Project Finalization Context
candidate_goal: Use the source to determine the documented ownership context for tote exchange setting updates and finalization.
summary: This candidate turns the source statement about customer updates and UAT finalization into a procedural check for documenting who is described as updating and finalizing tote exchange settings.
rough_steps: Review the Tote Exchange Settings training segment and note the statement about who can update the settings.
Record that the source says these settings can be updated by the customer.
Record that the source also says the settings are generally finalized with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT.
When documenting the configuration ownership context, distinguish between update capability and finalization context exactly as stated in the source.
Do not assign approval rules, access permissions, or escalation paths that are not explicitly provided by the source.
expected_result: The user has a source-backed note that tote exchange settings can be updated by the customer and are generally finalized with the project team and UPS at the end of UAT.
failure_or_escalation_notes: Escalate if a stronger ownership, approval, or permission statement is needed because this source does not define access control details.
Do not infer who should make production changes from this source alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training segment transcript or notes

source_refs.quote_or_summary: These can be updated by the customer, but for the most part we kind of finalize it with the project team with UPS at the end of UAT.

extraction_notes: This is a procedural reference candidate for documenting ownership context, not a permission model or change-management workflow.
Confidence is medium because the source gives a high-level statement but not a detailed procedure.
